title: Sweep Iterations: When Is the Posterior Done?

runtime.expected_outcome: Free-energy F drops sharply over the first 2-3 sweeps then plateaus. Most convergence is bought by the first pass.

symbols.q^(k): variational posterior at sweep iteration k

audiences.kid: When you think it over, the first couple of tries help the most. After that you're just polishing.
audiences.real: The perceive action sweeps until F converges. Diagnostic: F drops fast then plateaus; 2-3 sweeps captures most of the gain.
audiences.equation: Delta F_k decays geometrically for well-conditioned problems. Budget n_iters=2..4 to balance speed and accuracy.
audiences.derivation: Mean-field coordinate ascent on F monotonically decreases F. Standard VB convergence arguments (Bishop 2006, Ch 10) bound per-iteration improvement by a constant factor.

orchestrate.objective: Show that 2-3 sweep iterations capture most of the variational gain.
orchestrate.role: Convergence-budget tutor; practical calibration for n_iters.
orchestrate.context: tiny_open_goal, observe one obs, log Delta F over n_iters in {1..8}.

credits: Parr, Pezzulo & Friston (2022), Active Inference, MIT Press (CC BY-NC-ND), App B.
Bishop (2006), Pattern Recognition and Machine Learning, Ch 10.
